title: Book Essay — Fertility Holidays (Valeria)
html:
TL;DR

  • College book-review essay by Valeria (Yahir's sister) on Amy Speier's Fertility Holidays: IVF Tourism and the Reproduction of Whiteness (NYU Press, 2016)
  • Opens with the family's foundational fact: Mom was artificially inseminated via IVF on her birthday May 29, 2000 — Valeria born Feb 7, 2001 (also Dad's birthday)
  • Discloses a previously-uncatalogued family event: Valeria had a twin brother born alongside her who died in infancy
  • Confirms Mexican-immigrant parents paid IVF entirely out of pocket without health coverage — this is the financial origin of the "$40K" that surfaces in Dad's 2022 papers as the mi engaño grievance
  • Critiques Speier's research as unrepresentative (only Czech Republic, only white middle-class Americans) — early example of Valeria's analytical voice

Notes

December 2020 — academic essay by Valeria Gonzalez for a UO course (student ID redacted). The piece is technically a critique of an academic book on IVF tourism, but the autobiographical opening is the load-bearing content for the Gonzalez-family arc:

  1. IVF as conception story: Mom inseminated 2000-05-29 (her birthday); Valeria born 2001-02-07 (Dad's birthday). The symmetry was a family-pride point.
  2. Lost twin: Valeria had a twin brother who died in infancy. Not previously surfaced in the vault.
  3. Out-of-pocket IVF: As Mexican immigrants without coverage, the parents financed everything themselves — this is the real-world cost basis behind Dad's $40K for kids notation in the burned manuscript.

The Speier critique itself: Valeria thinks the book overgeneralizes from one country (Czech Republic) and one demographic (white middle-class Americans), misses India/Spain/etc., and thinks reproductive-tourism cost gaps ($25–45K US vs ~$10K Czech) are downstream of neoliberal healthcare and Foucauldian biopolitics.

This essay should be cross-linked with 2022 10 15 things on 10 1415 2022 — together they explain why "mi engaño" was Mom's infertility, not infidelity.
